query: Will EVs dominate Indian cities by 2032?

historical_match.name: South Korea EV rollout 2016-2022
historical_match.tipping_incident: 2019 Hyundai IONIQ subsidy tripling caused 300% YoY EV sales jump

probable.name: Gradual EV Dominance
probable.description: EV adoption reaches 30% market share in Indian cities by 2032 driven by PLI subsidies and falling battery costs.
probable.proof: Battery prices fell 90% from $1,100/kWh in 2010 to $110/kWh in 2024; India PLI allocated ₹18,100 crore for EV ecosystem.
probable.if_condition: Central government maintains PLI subsidies and public charging mandate through 2028
probable.but_condition: If oil prices drop below $50/barrel for 24+ months and remove cost advantage

plausible.name: Fragmented Transition
plausible.description: EV adoption splits sharply between top-10 cities (35% share) and tier-2 cities (under 5%) by 2032.
plausible.proof: Only 2.5% overall EV penetration in 2024 against India's 2030 target of 30%; charging stations concentrated 80% in 6 metro cities.
plausible.if_condition: Grid reliability improves in top-10 cities but rural electrification lags 4+ years behind
plausible.but_condition: Charging infrastructure gap in 600 smaller cities and 4,000+ towns remains structurally unfilled by 2032

possible.name: Disruption-Accelerated Surge
possible.description: A fuel price shock or solid-state battery breakthrough triggers sudden 50% EV adoption in cities by 2030.
possible.proof: China's EV market share hit 35% in 18 months after 2022 subsidy shock; solid-state battery pilots achieved 500km range in 2024.
possible.if_condition: Oil price spikes above $150/barrel for 6+ months OR solid-state battery reaches commercial scale by 2028
possible.but_condition: India's grid cannot absorb sudden 10× EV load without major blackouts across metro areas

preferable.name: Coordinated Green Transition
preferable.description: India achieves 40% EV penetration in cities by 2030 while building a clean grid and domestic cell manufacturing base.
preferable.proof: Norway hit 80% EV market share by 2023 starting from under 1% in 2010 via coordinated free-parking, toll exemptions, and grid investment.
preferable.needs_condition: Coordinated policy stack: PLI extension + mandatory charging infra in new construction + renewable grid buildout + local battery cell gigafactory
preferable.leverage: Mandate EV-only two-wheelers from 2027 — they represent 70% of India's total vehicle parc and 85% of urban trips

one_thing:
The decisive variable is not EV demand — it is charging station density.
Indians will buy EVs when they stop worrying about being stranded.
Demand is already there; the infrastructure is the bottleneck.
INCIDENT: Norway's 2013 free parking + charging policy drove 3× EV adoption jump in 12 months
WATCH: India charging station permits issued per quarter (target: 10,000/quarter by 2027)
IF YES → 30% EV penetration in cities by 2030
IF NO  → EV growth stalls at 8% despite ₹50,000 crore in subsidy spend
